profile.religion: NO PUBLIC PROFESSION FOUND. 30-year career educator/principal; campaign about-page uses 'love thy neighbor' as secular ethic. No cite-able church affiliation or Christ confession on record.
profile.education: B.S. Old Dominion University 1974; graduate degree University of Virginia 1979; doctoral degree Virginia Tech 1998.
profile.background: Mayor of Chesapeake VA (R, incumbent) — assumed office Nov 14 2017 after Alan Krasnoff resignation; won 2018 special 57.81%, won 2020 general 65.99%, RE-ELECTED Nov 5 2024 vs Don Carey III 55.7% to 43.9% (68,421 to 53,939 votes); current term ends January 1 2029. Career: 30-year classroom teacher and principal of four Chesapeake schools; school administrator 20+ years. Born and raised in rural Chesapeake; father U.S. Navy (often deployed); raised by mother and grandfather with two brothers and two sisters.

rick-west.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement — 9TH-CONSECUTIVE-DEFER RESOLVED] Candidacy CURRENT — re-elected Nov 5 2024 vs Don Carey III 55.7% to 43.9%; current term to Jan 1 2029. The 9-batch defer is resolved by completing candidacy verification PLUS cite-only scoring of a thin-but-anchored heterodox-R profile. NET: 1 HARD TRUE + 3 SOFT TRUE vs 2 HARD FALSE on 6 cite-able cells of 30; 24 NULL per cite-only discipline. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 — multi-cycle AFFIRMATIVE public-safety pay advocacy (campaign bio: 'as Mayor, West has ensured significant pay raises for public safety professionals, preventing staff shortages'; council eliminated curbside recycling specifically to fund public-safety pay; April 2025 attempted real-estate tax hike was specifically to fund fire/EMS/police — clears HIGH BAR for affirmative advocacy beyond routine budget vote). SOFT TRUE economic_stewardship q2 — April 2025 publicly announced proposed real-estate tax-rate increase 'no longer being considered' after public pushback; final budget used real-estate tax REBATE rather than rate hike (offsetting meals tax + curbside-recycling elimination). SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q0 — voted YES on unanimous Dec 10 2019 Chesapeake 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution after 5-hour hearing with 70 speakers (quoted: 'I believe you will leave here tonight with confidence that your council will do everything within our powers to preserve the rights granted to us by both the United States Constitution and the Constitution of Virginia'); HEDGE NOTED: council deliberately substituted 'constitutional' for 'sanctuary' on advice that 'sanctuary' could jeopardize city funding — watered down vs full sanctuary posture. SOFT TRUE self_defense q1 — same Dec 2019 2A-Constitutional-City resolution affirmed preservation of 2A rights against incoming Northam-era VA red-flag/AWB/UBC package (broad oppositional posture vs specific bill-level vote). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 — personally signed Chesapeake's FIRST-EVER Pride Month proclamation June 2024 (months before reelection), declaring 'LGBTQIA+ individuals have had immeasurable impact to the cultural, civic and economic successes of our community' and acknowledging Stonewall uprising; acknowledged from dais at city council meeting — R-MAYOR HETERODOX CROSS-PRESSURE FLAG (parallels Stafford BoS Diggs-bloc pattern, except West is the R-mayor signer rather than D majority). HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2 — multi-year COVID compliance posture; Nov 2022 official city video encouraging 'continue wearing a mask, practicing social distancing, and getting a vaccine when it's their turn'; led 'regular updates' supporting state guidance rather than challenging it; no Youngkin-era pivot to mask-rescind advocacy found (career CPS-administrator instinct visible). NULL/24 — all sanctity_of_life, family_child_sovereignty, christian_liberty (q0/q2/q3/q4 all NULL), election_integrity, border_immigration (mayor lacks jurisdiction; Sheriff Rosado runs ICE cooperation), self_defense q0, plus public_justice q1-q4 — NO cite-able statement located. Sparse-data UI flag applies (24 of 30 NULL); 9-batch defer specifically resolved by candidacy confirmation + Pride proclamation citation closing the most-loaded heterodox question. NO public faith profession is itself notable for a Republican mayor.

4.note: HARD: Personally signed Chesapeake's FIRST-EVER Pride Month proclamation June 2024 declaring 'LGBTQIA+ individuals have had immeasurable impact to the cultural, civic and economic successes of our community' and acknowledging Stonewall uprising; acknowledged proclamation from dais during city council meeting. Direction: affirmative LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy at mayoral level — R-mayor heterodox cross-pressure.

2.note: SOFT: April 2025 publicly announced proposed real-estate tax-rate increase 'no longer being considered' after public pushback; final budget used real-estate tax REBATE rather than rate hike. Pattern of rate-hike-avoidance though shifted to meals tax + curbside-recycling cut. Direction: opposes tax-rate increases though not orthodox balanced-budget framework.

0.note: HARD multi-cycle: Campaign bio 'as Mayor, West has ensured significant pay raises for public safety professionals, preventing staff shortages'; council eliminated curbside recycling specifically to fund public-safety pay plan; 2025 attempted real-estate hike was specifically to fund fire/EMS/police. AFFIRMATIVE pro-police advocacy clearing HIGH BAR (not just routine budget vote).

SOFT: Voted YES on unanimous Dec 10 2019 Chesapeake 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution after 5-hour hearing with 70 speakers; on-dais quote 'I believe you will leave here tonight with confidence that your council will do everything within our powers to preserve the rights granted to us by both the United States Constitution and the Constitution of Virginia.' HEDGE: deliberately substituted 'constitutional' for 'sanctuary' to preserve state funding.

No Youngkin-era pivot to mask-rescind advocacy found. Did NOT protect citizens from state-overreach mandate framework.

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY STATED on campaign site, blog posts, social media, or news coverage. No church affiliation, no faith-language, no biographical 'raised in' anchor surfaced.
profile.education: B.A. Washington State University; M.Ed. Special Education (George Mason University); M.Ed. Educational Leadership (William & Mary).
profile.background: Stafford County (VA) School Board, Aquia District (D); first-term sworn January 1 2026 (term to Dec 31 2029). Defeated Annette Scharpenberg (R) Nov 4 2025: 4,319 (51.47%) vs 4,017 (47.87%); replaces Maya Guy (who ran successfully for Aquia BoS). 19th-year public school teacher (special education); currently at Chantilly HS (Fairfax County) where he is varsity girls' basketball head coach; previously taught Stafford 11 years (Colonial Forge HS 4 yrs + Brooke Point HS 7 yrs). Resident Aquia district 14 years; four children, two graduated from Stafford public schools. Endorsed by Stafford Democratic Committee.

josh-regan.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement — PROMOTED roster_verified → evidence_local] Cite-only discipline applied to freshman D SB-Aquia 5-month tenure (Jan-June 2026). NET: 0 TRUE + 1 HARD FALSE + 29 NULL of 30. The dominant signal is correctly NULL by design — Regan ran a 'deliberately narrow on-instruction-only' campaign (campaign blog posts content-neutral: data-driven instruction methodology + 'I, if elected, and the board as a whole, cannot institute a policy that runs counter to established Virginia law' governance-focused explainer); his predecessor's controversial votes (Guy's 2022 Pride flag motion, 2019 SOGI 4-3, Vlaming amicus, COVID masks) all PREDATE his tenure; opponent Scharpenberg owned the conservative-contrast lane Regan refused to engage. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — voted YES on 2/25/2026 preliminary $529.3M FY27 SCPS budget 6-1 (Healy sole NO) and 4/30/2026 final $525.9M FY27 budget unanimous; ~5% YoY growth over $503M FY26 baseline plus $10.6M new-school operating + $16.2M employee compensation. KEY CORRECTION/CLARIFICATION: the much-cited 6/3/2026 5-1 Stafford Pride proclamation was a BOARD OF SUPERVISORS action NOT School Board — Regan as SB member was NOT in that voting body and cannot be scored on it; the companion 6/4/2025 BoS proclamation predates his tenure. No SCPS Pride proclamation surfaced during his 5-month tenure. SCPS nondiscrimination policy revisit appears scheduled for September 2026 (search-hit, requires confirmation) — OUTSIDE current evidence window; recommend rescore pass October 2026 for first scorable biblical_marriage q4 + family_child_sovereignty q2 + refuse_state_overreach q3 opportunity. Religion: NOT publicly stated (no church affiliation, no faith-language, no biographical 'raised in' anchor, unlike Fingerholz). VPAP committee 535470 'Regan for Stafford County School Board - Joshua' returned 403 in research — donor/PAC detail (including potential MVPACE/VEA disclosure) was not retrievable; MVPACE endorsement NOT documented (unlike Fingerholz). iVoterGuide questionnaire NOT located (likely declined). Coaching context: head girls' varsity basketball coach at Chantilly HS — relevant if Title IX/transgender-athlete questions surface; no public statement on this issue yet. Sparse-data UI flag does NOT apply at extreme level (1 HARD FALSE + 29 NULL is honest for a 5-month freshman with a deliberately narrow campaign).

2.note: HARD: Voted YES on 2/25/2026 preliminary $529.3M FY27 SCPS budget 6-1 (Healy sole NO) + 4/30/2026 final $525.9M FY27 budget unanimous. ~5% YoY growth over $503M FY26 baseline + $10.6M new-school operating + $16.2M employee compensation. On majority side of hike-the-ask vote.

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED. No church or personal faith affiliation found in primary or secondary sources. Attended University of San Diego (a Catholic institution) for undergrad but USD attendance is institutional only — NEVER infer personal Catholic faith from institutional context per cite-only discipline.
profile.education: Hawthorne Elementary + James Madison HS (San Diego); B.A. University of San Diego (Catholic institution) — served as student body president.
profile.background: Mayor of San Diego CA (D, second term) — sworn Dec 10 2024 at 75th Inauguration Ceremony at Jacobs Music Center after defeating retired SDPD officer Larry Turner ~55.4% to 44.6% in Nov 2024; current term through Dec 2028. Prior: SD City Council D3 (2008-2016, Council President 2012; interim Mayor Aug 2013-Mar 2014 after Bob Filner resignation), CA Assembly AD-78 (2016-2020, Majority Whip 2018). FIRST openly LGBTQ+ person and FIRST person of color elected mayor of San Diego. Born May 10 1978. Heritage: Filipino, Dutch, Puerto Rican, Native American; enrolled member Tlingit & Haida Indian Tribes of Alaska. Lives downtown San Diego with partner Adam Smith and dog Diego.

todd-gloria.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Classic-orthodox-D LOCAL profile. NET: 1 HARD TRUE + 2 SOFT TRUE vs 14 HARD FALSE + 3 SOFT FALSE on 20 cite-able cells of 30; 10 NULL per cite-only discipline. 14 HARD FALSE multi-vector cross-anchor: sanctity_of_life q0 (Dobbs response 'very dark day') + q1 (Oct 2022 press conference IN FRONT of Planned Parenthood with PP President Darrah Johnson endorsing SD City Council resolution on Prop 1; June 2024 buffer-zone ordinance banning pro-life sidewalk-counselors within 8 ft); biblical_marriage q0 (openly gay; lives with male partner Adam Smith) + q2 (Aug 2023 REMOVED Pastor Dennis Hodges from Citizens Advisory Board on Police/Community Relations after Hodges abstained from 'condemn transphobia' letter on Christian conviction — Gloria memo: Hodges 'has made repeated concerning public comments about LGBTQ people — specifically, the transgender community'; Hodges' federal §1983 suit pending) + q4 (June 7 2021 RAISED Progress Pride Flag over San Diego City Hall for FIRST time in city history with quote 'The rainbow flag flying proudly at City Hall for the first time sends an important message to our LGBTQ community: we see you, we support you and you belong here'; created standing Mayoral LGBTQ Advisory Group; launched city-funded Pride Promenade construction); christian_liberty q0 + q2 (same Hodges removal cross-anchor — the predicate act was refusing to sign 'condemn transphobia' letter on biblical conscience; classic religious-conscience-vs-LGBT-affirmation collision) + q4 (Pride flag raising + Pride Promenade public-works investment + Prop 1 city resolution); refuse_state_overreach q0+q1+q2+q3 (Dec 1 2021 imposed vaccine mandate on ALL city employees with statement 'the proposed vaccine mandate for City employees is an issue of public safety for both the community and employees'; 629 officers initially unvaccinated, 130 LEFT THE FORCE; Aug 2 2021 indoor mask rule for all City of Phoenix facilities; July 2025 EO explicitly 'reaffirms the City's alignment with SB 54' Calif. Values Act — affirmatively aligned WITH state framework); border_immigration q1+q2 (July 2025 Executive Order: SDPD 'does not and shall not engage in immigration enforcement'; prohibits sharing license-plate-reader data; Oct 2025 City Council passed Due Process & Safety Ordinance Act codifying EO; 'deeply upset' by ICE operation at SD Italian restaurant); self_defense q0+q1 (Sep 2021 signed E.N.U.F. Ordinance banning possession/purchase/sale/receipt/transportation of non-serialized firearms and unfinished frames/receivers; misdemeanor / $1,000 / 6 months in jail; defended against Firearms Policy Coalition + SD County Gun Owners PAC suit). 3 SOFT FALSE: family_child_sovereignty q2 (SD Public Library hosts annual 'Pride Storytime' branded under Drag Queen Story Hour SD, city-funded LGBTQIA+ Library Services program, no parental-opt-out posture from mayor); economic_stewardship q2 (FY26 budget faced $258M structural deficit; Voice of San Diego retrospective headlined 'Disappointment Follows Gloria Into Sixth Year'; mixed record — uses cuts/efficiencies but persistent gap large); public_justice q4 NULL (DEI-office-creation does NOT cleanly map to Rom 13:4 sword cell; held NULL not FALSE per cite-only). HETERODOX CATCHES: HARD TRUE public_justice q2 — June 29 2023 signed Unsafe Camping Ordinance with on-dais quote 'there has to be consequences for illegal behavior in the city'; 3-encounter progressive enforcement with misdemeanor escalation — unusually firm for coastal-D mayor, drew progressive activist protest at his office. SOFT TRUE public_justice q3 — FY26 SDPD +4% YoY ($702.3M); May 2025 revised budget restored Northwestern Division patrols. SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q4 — Feb 2023 proposed ending COVID-19 emergency declaration + sunsetting city employee vax mandate in step with state. DEI-OFFICE-PARADOX flag: created standalone Chief Race & Equity Officer position Aug 2021 BUT proposed eliminating Race & Equity Office in 2025 amid deficit (folding into HR for ~$460K savings) + VETOED council attempt to restore it June 2025 — fiscal pragmatism > DEI orthodoxy in this one cell. Net SOFT FALSE because he created it in the first place. Religion NULL: no church/faith affiliation found; USD attendance institutional only.

profile.religion: Jewish (Reform). Member of Congregation Emanu-El, a Reform synagogue in San Francisco. Son of Rabbi Brian Lurie (former executive director of the Jewish Community Federation of San Francisco) and Mimi (Ruchwarger) Lurie. NOT CHRISTIAN — christian_liberty cells partial direct-action scoring only per Jewish-mayor protocol.
profile.education: Town School for Boys + University High School (SF) → B.A. Political Science Duke University 1999 → M.P.P. Goldman School of Public Policy UC Berkeley 2005.
profile.background: 46th Mayor of the City and County of San Francisco CA (D, first term) — sworn Jan 8 2025 after defeating incumbent London Breed in Nov 5 2024 RCV race ~56%-43% in final round; current term to Jan 2029. Heir to Levi Strauss fortune via stepfather Peter E. Haas (Haas family); first cousin once removed of U.S. Rep. Dan Goldman (D-NY). Personal net worth est. $8-47M (SF Chronicle Feb 2025). Career: Bill Bradley 2000 IA field organizer → Robin Hood Foundation NYC 2001 → Founded Tipping Point Community 2005 (raised >$500M, stepped down as CEO Nov 2019 remained board chair) → chaired SF 2016 Super Bowl Host Committee. Married Becca Prowda 2006 (she is CA Director of Protocol under Gov. Newsom); two children. Born 1977. Self-funded ~$8M+ of ~$16M total campaign; FIRST SF mayor elected with no prior government experience since 1911. CAPPED OWN PAY at $1/year (declined full $364,582 mayoral salary).

daniel-lurie.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HETERODOX-MODERATE-D crossover — ~17 months in office; sparse-data UI flag applies. NET: 6 HARD TRUE + 5 HARD FALSE + 3 SOFT FALSE on 14 cite-able cells of 30; 16 NULL per cite-only discipline. UNUSUAL 5-OF-5 HARD TRUE public_justice cluster: q0 'Rebuilding the Ranks' executive directive 5/13/2025 promised 425 new sworn officers + sheriff deputies + 911 dispatchers in first 3 years; signed new SFPOA contract; ran on 'fully-staffed police department'; Frank Jordan (former SF police chief + mayor) endorsed him on this basis — affirmative back-the-blue clearing HIGH BAR. q1 public partnership with DA Brooke Jenkins (who replaced recalled Chesa Boudin); jointly rejected federal-troops intervention while citing 25% citywide crime drop + lowest homicide rate since 1950s; mayoral office formally thanked Jenkins for 'partnership' on fentanyl. q2 directed SFPD + DPW to clear encampments (tents reduced 324 → 162 by Dec 2025); 6/26/2025 drug-market blitz = 97 arrests in one day (largest in recent SF history); 11/24/2025 expanded late-night Tenderloin drug raids with quote 'another step forward in our city's fight against open-air drug markets and crime.' q3 SF.gov 'Mayor Lurie Ends Distribution of Fentanyl Smoking Supplies Without Counseling and Treatment' — broke directly from prior SF harm-reduction policy. q4 signed Fentanyl State of Emergency Ordinance Feb 2025 (10-1 BoS vote); 3/17/2025 'Breaking the Cycle' executive directive; 822 Geary St. 24/7 stabilization center. PLUS HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 — capped own salary at $1/yr; FY26-27 $16.9B budget proposal closes $643M deficit with $300M structural cut (eliminates 550 vacant positions; deep nonprofit-contract trims, 1,050+ projected nonprofit layoffs); 12/12/2025 ordered $400M in mid-year cuts; inauguration line 'stop spending more than we can afford.' 5 HARD FALSE: biblical_marriage q0 (campaign LGBTQ+ platform pledges to 'ensure San Francisco remains a safe city for trans students'; hosted Pride proclamation 6/6/2025 City Hall) + q2 (6/6/2025 Pride proclamation quote 'At a time when LGBTQ+ rights are under attack across the country, in San Francisco we are sending a different message: You are welcome here, you are seen here, you are safe here') + q4 (Marched in 2025 SF Pride Parade on Market St. 6/29/2025); border_immigration q1 (Direct quote 6/13/2025: 'For decades, San Francisco has had sanctuary policies — that means our police officers and city staff do not assist with federal immigration enforcement' and 'these policies make our city safer and that is what I was elected to do and that is why I stand by them 100%') + q2 (Oct 2025 activated Dept of Emergency Management amid CBP-surge reports; directed local LE 'to support immigrant communities, protect peaceful protesters and refrain from assisting with federal civil immigration enforcement'; quote 'These actions are not meant to fix our broken immigration system, they are intended to terrorize people'). 3 SOFT FALSE: family_child_sovereignty q2 (Dream Keeper equity program continuation as 'RFP 100' approved $36M to 'advance equity'); christian_liberty q4 (9/30/2025 hosted PRC Consul General flag-raising at City Hall, proclaimed 10/1/2025 'China-US Friendship and Heritage Day' — state-sponsored PRC National Day endorsement); self_defense q1 (gun-storage program launch — Pierce Hu memorial; Gun Violence Awareness Day proclamation; 1-day ceasefire announcement). JEWISH-Reform mayor — christian_liberty q0/q2/q3 cells legitimately NULL because not personally Christian and no specifically Christian-institution case identified during tenure. SFUSD-jurisdiction caveat — family_child_sovereignty q0/q1 NULL because SFUSD governed by independent elected board. SPARSE-DATA: 17 months in office; recommend rescore pass at 2-year mark Jan 2027. HETERODOX FLAGS catalog: (1) $1 salary; (2) $643M deficit-closure budget; (3) Fentanyl State of Emergency Ordinance 10-1 BoS; (4) ended fentanyl-supply distribution without treatment requirement; (5) Rebuilding the Ranks 425-officer directive; (6) 97-arrest drug-market blitz + expanded Tenderloin raids; (7) public DA partnership with Brooke Jenkins; (8) encampment 324→162 in year one; (9) endorsed by SF Briones Society (R-leaning); (10) condemned 'Tax the Jews' chant Feb 2026; (11) vocally opposed federal-troops intervention; (12) PRC flag-raising 9/30/2025 + China-US Friendship Day. SIX major back-the-blue/fiscal-restraint/anti-permissiveness moves — the brand.

2.note: HARD: 6/6/2025 City Hall Pride proclamation quote 'At a time when LGBTQ+ rights are under attack across the country, in San Francisco we are sending a different message: You are welcome here, you are seen here, you are safe here.' profile.religion: Ordained Baptist minister, Reverend. Historically-Black Baptist tradition (NBC-USA orbit). Preaches Sundays at Bethlehem Missionary Baptist Church Richmond CA (her husband Rev. Dr. Alvin C. Bernstine's pulpit since 2006). Teaches Christian Theology and Black Theology at the Leadership Institute at Allen Temple (LIAT) — Allen Temple Baptist Church Oakland — since 2015; holds Professor of Theological Studies title. M.T.S. Pacific School of Religion (Berkeley, progressive ecumenical seminary) 2015. NOT Disciples of Christ; NOT COGIC; NOT Pepperdine — prior brief was incorrect on denomination, JD, and church.
profile.education: B.A. Economics, San Francisco State University; J.D. Golden Gate University School of Law; M.T.S. (Master of Theological Studies), Pacific School of Religion 2015.
profile.background: District Attorney, Contra Costa County CA (D, second-term incumbent) — appointed Sept 2017 by BoS to fill Mark Peterson vacancy; won 2018 first-election majority no-runoff; RE-ELECTED June 7 2022 56% vs Mary Knox (37-yr veteran Deputy DA); current term through Jan 2027 (next election 2028). Born Aug 16 1951 (age 74). Resident El Sobrante CA. Prior: 22 years Contra Costa Superior Court judge (appointed 1995 by Gov. Pete Wilson — a REPUBLICAN appointment); elected Presiding Judge 2011; past president National Association of Women Judges. Two adult sons from prior marriage. Married Rev. Dr. Alvin C. Bernstine 2020 (senior pastor Bethlehem MBC Richmond). RECALL EFFORT: filed paperwork March 2025, missed Sept 25 2025 signature deadline (needed 72,556 valid sigs), officially ended October 2025.
